briefing.opening: Good morning. I will now begin the climate briefing for June 17, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Climate Crisis and Increased Vulnerability in the Asian Region
sections.content: The World Meteorological Organization (WMO) has warned that Asian countries are suffering severe economic damage due to climate change, and that the pace of climate change in the Asian region is accelerating faster than the global average, leading to record-high ocean heat [1, 2, 19]. This climate crisis is exposing half of the world's children to complex climate threats such as heatwaves, droughts, and floods, with children in Mongolian ger villages and residents of vulnerable countries like Sudan experiencing direct suffering [5, 12, 29, 132, 133, 137]. Domestically, cultural heritage, such as the Nangwondaejeonsa Pagoda Stele in Gangneung, is being damaged by cold waves due to climate change [3], and analysis suggests that the Jeju region has already transformed into a subtropical climate due to heatwaves and extreme heavy rainfall [15]. Furthermore, climate change is increasing the occurrence of sudden pest outbreaks [11], and 'super' El Niño events are heightening the risk of exacerbating extreme weather and increasing the potential spread of new and deadly microorganisms, thereby threatening human survival [127, 128].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Carbon Neutrality Practices and Local Community Efforts
sections.content: Efforts to practice carbon neutrality in response to the climate crisis are actively being pursued by various domestic and international entities. POSCO Group is leading the transition to carbon-neutral steel by operating the nation's largest electric arc furnace, aiming to achieve carbon neutrality by 2050 [34, 38, 47, 69], while Hyundai Motor and Kia are accelerating their transition to eco-friendly mobility, having received a 'transition company' rating in global electric vehicle transition assessments [22]. Local governments nationwide are encouraging citizens to practice carbon-neutral living through various programs, including Environment Day ceremonies, carbon-neutral climate camps, resource circulation experiential education, plastic exchange centers, and the operation of carbon-neutral point systems [28, 31, 32, 35, 36, 37, 39, 40, 41, 42, 43, 44, 46, 48, 50, 51, 52, 54, 55, 56, 57, 58, 60, 61, 62, 63, 64, 65, 66, 67, 68, 71, 73, 75, 77, 78, 79, 80, 81, 82]. The French Agency for Ecological Transition (ADEME) has also launched its Carbon Neutrality Scenario Season 2, strengthening the national carbon neutrality roadmap [141].

sections.title: ⚠️ Opportunities and Challenges of Renewable Energy Transition
sections.content: The transition to renewable energy continues, but new challenges are emerging in the process. Korea Southern Power (KOSPO) is promoting its main power source transition by supplying 11.2 GW of renewable energy by 2040 and launching the 'Renewable Energy Business Development Strategic Advisory Council' [84, 86, 87, 94, 95, 98, 101, 103, 106, 107, 110, 111, 113, 114, 118], and Hana Bank is actively building a renewable energy investment network, including arranging financing for the nation's largest offshore wind power project [88, 99, 116, 123]. However, a move by some companies to scale back their energy transition plans has been observed, with oil major Equinor abandoning its 2030 renewable energy targets and allocating only 10% of its power investments to renewables [89, 100]. Furthermore, facing a surge in power demand from Artificial Intelligence (AI) data centers, South Korea is experiencing a dual challenge in securing electricity due to the intermittency of renewable energy [90, 93], and the International Energy Agency (IEA) has pointed out Southeast Asia's excessive reliance on fossil fuels, urging the expansion of renewable energy and nuclear power [117, 119]. The UN Secretary-General also emphasized the need to accelerate the global transition, calling for a rapid shift to renewable energy at the World Summit [105].

sections.title: 📰 Climate Change Research, Awareness Spreading, and Societal Response
sections.content: Efforts to enhance scientific understanding and spread social awareness of climate change are ongoing. Scientists are conducting research on the impact of climate change on ocean circulation and the survival potential of coral reefs [125, 126], and discussions continue regarding the potential for developing heat and drought-resistant crop varieties to aid in climate change response [130]. Domestically, changes and responses are emerging in daily life, such as Lotte Home Shopping expanding products for heatwaves and monsoon seasons in line with changing shopping trends due to climate change, and the Korea Meteorological Administration conducting heatwave prevention campaigns for mobile workers [6, 16, 21, 24, 25]. Meanwhile, discussions are active regarding the social aspects of climate change, such as its connection to patriarchy and the inadequacy of traditional legal systems [134, 135, 136, 139], and there is a growing call for climate change to demand concrete action, not just mere hope [140].

briefing.closing: I hope today's briefing serves as an opportunity to recognize the severity of the climate crisis and urge action from all of us. This concludes the climate briefing for June 17, 2026.
